prompt: Construct a VenueReserve Operations Suite supporting an offline-first fitness and wellness studio that sells memberships, class packs, and branded merchandise while managing room, equipment, and seat reservations. The Vue.js front end provides role-based dashboards for Members, Front Desk Staff, Studio Managers, Moderators, and Auditors. Members can browse availability by date and time, add one or more reservations to a cart, and see real-time price recalculation as they apply studio-defined coupons (for example, $10 off purchases over $75, or 15% off a class pack with a maximum discount of $25). Reservation rules are configurable per resource: a standard booking window of 30 days out, minimum duration of 30 minutes, maximum duration of 4 hours, 10-minute early check-in allowance, 15-minute late check-in grace period before marking as no-show, and automatic stitching of adjacent 30-minute slots into a single continuous reservation when policy allows. Holiday-specific rules (such as reduced hours on 07/04) appear in the calendar, and exceptions require Manager approval with an on-screen reason prompt. During scheduling, the UI highlights conflicts with plain-language causes (capacity exceeded, occupancy overlap, required 15-minute cleaning buffer, or blocked resource) and supports both auto-suggested time slots and manual adjustments. The same interface also includes a community feed where posts and replies may be submitted, held for review when flagged, and reported by users with visible status feedback.The backend uses Koa to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js app over the local network, with MySQL as the system of record for reservations, resources, users, orders, refunds, and audit trails. The reservation engine evaluates rules at write time, including blacklist restrictions and an internal account standing score (for example, users below 60/100 or with two no-shows in the last 30 days are blocked from peak hours unless approved). Order processing follows an explicit state machine with idempotent placement using a client-generated idempotency key; unpaid orders auto-cancel after 15 minutes, returning inventory and reservation holds. A unified order model supports new memberships, renewals, class purchases, and merchandise in one cart, with order split/merge when items require different fulfillment (instant activation vs. front-desk pickup). Because the system must run fully offline, “payment method management” is limited to local tender types (cash, external card terminal, gift certificate) and storing only masked references and last four digits when entered manually; no online payment or third-party services are used. Moderation includes automated throttles (for example, max 10 posts per hour per account and optional IP/device fingerprint limits), local CAPTCHA challenges, allowlists/blocklists, and a report console to accept, reject, or ban with durations like 24 hours or permanent. Security controls include salted password hashing, parameter validation, optional anti-replay timestamps on sensitive APIs, sensitive-data masking in logs, anomaly alerts for repeated failed logins (lock for 15 minutes after 8 failures), and queryable security events. Financial logs are tamper-evident via chained hashes, and reconciliation produces daily and per-shift reports with variance flags when the counted drawer differs from expected totals by more than $5.00, preserving a complete audit trail for check-ins, fines, refunds, and permission changes.
